headline: Childcare Costs Consume 28% of Dual-Income B40 Household Earnings
context: Women, Family and Community Development Ministry data showed that formal childcare costs averaged RM850 per child monthly in urban areas in 2025, consuming up to 28% of dual-income B40 household earnings and forcing many mothers out of the workforce.

cards.big: RM850 per child monthly for childcare — B40 dual-income couples spend 28% of combined earnings just on daycare
cards.sub: 42% of B40 mothers leave workforce after second child because childcare costs exceed their salary

cards.big: Female labour force participation at 56.2% is lowest in ASEAN — childcare costs are the single largest barrier cited by 48% of women
cards.sub: Each 1% increase in female participation adds RM8.2 billion to GDP — childcare barrier costs economy RM45 billion annually

cards.big: Only 4,800 registered childcare centres serve 14.2 million working-age adults — 1 centre per 2,958 potential users
cards.sub: Unregistered home-based centres estimated at 28,000 — parents choose cheaper unlicensed options due to affordability crisis

cards.big: Government childcare subsidy of RM180 monthly covers only 21% of average urban childcare costs
cards.sub: Japan spends 1.6% of GDP on childcare subsidies versus Malaysia's 0.08% — 20x gap in public investment

cards.big: Telling women to work while making childcare unaffordable isn't empowerment — it's a policy contradiction that hurts families and the economy
Should Malaysia provide universal free childcare for B40 and M40 households funded through a 0.5% employer levy?
